pairs.archetype_description: Four-axis anti-AI: GPT-impossible opener, obscure company reference, non-formula structure, front-loaded typo.
pairs.what_to_notice: Which email feels like it was written by a real person? Which one would you actually reply to?

version_a.subject_line: a question about yale new haven's fiscal 2025 losses
version_a.email_body:
Hi Jennifer,

Saw reports that Yale New Haven Health closed fiscal 2025 down nearly $200 million, with Medicaid shortfalls over $430M and a new CFO just stepping in this March. Strategic operations at a five-hospital system carrying that kind of pressure tends to mean every dollar of coding accuracy matters more than it did 12 months ago.

One Director at a similar academic system cut denial rates 27% and recovered cash flow without adding to the billing team.

Directors use autonomous coding to bring coding costs down 40% while holding accuracy above 98%. Curious whether that math is worth a closer look for Yale New Haven's setup?

version_b.email_body:
dont know if this lands Jennifer, but the Wallingford urgent care opening post you put up caught me at a weird time.

Five hospitals, a $200M loss year, Medicaid shortfall north of $430M, and you're standing up new outpatient footprint anyway. That's a strategic ops job most people wouldn't want.

The coding side of a build-while-bleeding year is where the quiet money hides. One similar system found 27% of their denials were coming from the newest sites, not the ones they assumed.

Anyway. If Arnim's first 90 days include a look at coding accuracy, I'd love to be in that conversation.

pairs.archetype_description: 3-4 lines, ~50 words. Signal-led opener, what we do, the ask. LinkedIn-DM-short.
pairs.what_to_notice: Would the crisp 3-4 line version get a reply where the longer one gets ignored? Or does it feel too thin?

version_a.subject_line: a question about uw medicine's 2026 payer changes
version_a.email_body:
Hi Nikki,

Saw UW Medicine is out of Regence, Humana, PacificSource, and Wellpoint Medicare Advantage networks for 2026, on top of the federal funding uncertainty hitting clinical trials and research. Operations and finance under that kind of payer shift usually means risk management is absorbing denial pressure the rest of the system doesn't see yet.

One Director at a similar academic system cut denial rates 27% during a payer renegotiation year without adding coders.

Directors use autonomous coding to hold accuracy above 98% while payer mix shifts underneath them. Curious whether that approach fits what UW Medicine is navigating right now?

version_b.email_body:
Hi Nikki, UW Medicine is out of four Medicare Advantage networks for 2026 on top of federal funding uncertainty.

We help academic systems hold accuracy above 98% and cut denials 27% during payer shifts.

Open to a quick call?

pairs.archetype_description: 4 sentences, 55-70 words. Problem, solution, proof, CTA.
pairs.what_to_notice: Which length feels right for a cold email from someone you don't know?

version_a.subject_line: a question about agrace's wauwatosa expansion
version_a.email_body:
Hi Carrie,

Saw Agrace is extending into Ozaukee, Washington, Racine, and Kenosha out of the new Wauwatosa regional office while the $30M Dementia Village campaign keeps building. Regional operations stretched across new counties plus a capital project usually means the billing team is getting pulled thinner than anyone's naming out loud.

One Director at a similar hospice cut coding costs 40% during a regional expansion without growing their billing team.

Directors use autonomous coding to keep accuracy above 98% while operations scale. Worth a 15 minute look at how that would apply to Agrace's southeast Wisconsin rollout?

version_b.email_body:
Hi Carrie, Agrace's Wauwatosa expansion plus the Dementia Village build typically stretches the billing team long before the counties are fully live.

We help hospice Directors cut coding costs 40% and keep accuracy above 98% during expansions.

One regional hospice pulled two billing reqs after seeing the numbers.

Open to a short call this week?

pairs.archetype_description: Anti-AI opener with an obscure reference and front-loaded typo vs a 3-line signal-led DM-short email. Which feels more human?
pairs.what_to_notice: Which opening signal hooks you more? Describe in one word what caught your eye in the winning version.

version_a.email_body:
random one Joe, but the 2026 sliding scale going live on January 15 is the sort of line item nobody writes a memo about.

running rev cycle and physician admin at a place that just took a BGCF grant for NourishBG while updating charity tiers means your coding team is the one who has to make the numbers tie out at the claim level.

what usually breaks in that setup is not the policy, its the codes.

dont need a reply, just wanted to flag this before your Q2 close.

version_b.email_body:
Joe, the Women's Choice outpatient recognition usually comes with rev cycle teams stretched to match the volume.

One peer VP cut A/R days 30% without adding coders.

Worth a 15 minute chat?

pairs.archetype_description: Anti-AI typed-fast human vs the current THE ONE framework. Which voice earns the reply?
pairs.what_to_notice: Which opening signal hooks you more? Describe in one word what caught your eye in the winning version.

version_a.email_body:
dont know if the S.E.E. Project funding loss is the one that hurts most Joanne, or if its one of the other three.

515 employees, a $50m January 2027 target, and finance running cover on all of it means the coding and billing side of the house is probably the last thing getting attention and the first thing leaking.

one VP at a similar sized nonprofit network found that 30% of their A/R days were sitting in coder reviewable claims nobody had cycles for.

if that is a useful conversation ahead of FY26, I am around.

version_b.subject_line: a question about mar monte's fy26 setup
version_b.email_body:
Hi Joanne,

Saw Planned Parenthood Mar Monte is losing $6.5m a month in reimbursable care while standing up a $50m raise target by January 2027, with California's $90m state backfill only partially covering the federal cuts. Finance teams running that kind of double pressure usually find the quietest leaks in the coding and billing workflow.

One VP at a similar multi site nonprofit network cut denial rates 27% without adding a coder.

VPs use autonomous coding to hold accuracy above 98% while cost to collect comes down 70%. Worth 15 minutes to see if that would apply to Mar Monte?

pairs.archetype_description: 3-line signal-crisp DM vs an anonymized peer story. Brevity or narrative?
pairs.what_to_notice: Which opening signal hooks you more? Describe in one word what caught your eye in the winning version.

version_a.email_body:
Jessica, saw the associate's degree post, congrats on getting it across the line alongside revenue integrity work.

We help directors cut coding costs 40% while holding accuracy above 98%.

Open to a 15 minute chat?

version_b.subject_line: how a peer hospital handled non expansion pressure
version_b.email_body:
Hi Jessica,

A Director of Revenue Integrity at a similar safety net hospital in a non expansion state walked me through their FY25 reset.

They assumed denials were a payer problem. Audit showed 27% were coder preventable, buried in charity and self pay claims. After switching the coding workflow, accuracy went to 98% and coding costs came down 40%, without adding a single coder to the team.

Nashville General carrying a fresh audit note plus Tennessee's Medicaid stance is the same starting line they ran from.

Worth 15 minutes to compare what they did?

pairs.archetype_description: Anti-AI human vs signal-crisp, second signal pairing for variance.
pairs.what_to_notice: Which opening signal hooks you more? Describe in one word what caught your eye in the winning version.

version_a.email_body:
dont know if this is a fair email to send the day after a women's basketball championship partnership drops, Melanie, but here we are.

ortho groups scaling brand and sports med partnerships usually discover six months in that their coding throughput is the actual limit on growth, not clinic capacity.

Castle Connolly calling OrthoVirginia #1 in the state plus the A-10 deal is the kind of year where the rev cycle team becomes the bottleneck nobody named yet.

happy to walk through what usually breaks first.

version_b.email_body:
Melanie, the A-10 women's basketball partnership is the kind of visibility year where coding volume runs ahead of coder hiring.

We help ortho groups cut A/R days 30% while keeping accuracy above 98%.

Worth a chat?
